headline: Small S windswell with clean cross-off wind — log it while it's glassy-ish.
today: Short-period S windswell in the 2ft range, so nothing with much punch, but the WNW/NW cross-off keeps it clean all day. Get on it in the morning around the incoming tide before the midday high fattens things out. Log or groveler only — this is a play-around session, not a mission.

daysToWatch.note: Swell filling in but SE cross-on all morning turning onshore 19mph by evening — junky and building. Skip unless you like it messy.
The one to circle: 3-4ft SSE with WNW cross-off holding through the afternoon. Bring the shortboard, this is worth rearranging plans.
Leftover 2-3ft with a clean cross-off dawn window before it goes onshore in the afternoon. Early or nothing.
